focus: Q10 HVR pitfalls audit: 3 worst patterns to avoid in child-002 rewrite (semicolons in complex sentences, Oxford commas in lists, em dashes for emphasis)

answer: 3 worst HVR pitfalls: (1) Semicolons in complex explanatory sentences - both READMEs extensively use semicolons to join technical clauses; (2) Oxford commas in lists - Public root README uses Oxford commas in high-visibility list contexts; (3) Em dashes for emphasis - both READMEs use em dashes in technical descriptions. System-code-graph README should prioritize short simple sentences, bullet-point lists, and clean prose style from HVR-compliant reference docs.
